title: Narrative Arc — Security Automation Needs Guardrails
recognition: Security automation promises faster triage, enrichment, correlation, recommendation, and remediation.
reframe: Faster response also increases the speed and reach of mistakes.
framework: Allowed actions, required approvals, forbidden actions, scope, escalation, rollback, least privilege, and audit evidence define the guardrails.
proof: The draft distinguishes speed from safety and explicitly holds technical claims for current verification.
application: Security leaders can govern automation as an operating capability with authority proportionate to consequence.
consequence: Well-designed guardrails make automation more trustworthy and reviewable rather than merely more powerful.
invitation: Design boundaries as deliberately as capabilities before trusting security automation.
